name: Real failing case — v15 instructed £696.14 further Nominee draw without checking cash
description: This is the case that exposed the v15 bug. Contributions £1,237.43, retained £848.00 (locked model), distributable £389.43, 30% to creditors = £116.83 required, paid £5.06 → shortfall £111.77. The v15 instruction called for: draw further £696.14 to Nominee fee, then pay £111.77 to creditors (total outflow £807.91). The v16 waterfall must prevent the £696.14 draw and instead check whether cash in hand is sufficient to pay the shortfall directly. Full R&P figures to be populated from the actual case documents — placeholder figures used below pending manual supply.

inputs.status: PARTIAL — full R&P figures required from case documents
outstanding_data_needed: Total fees drawn to Nominee (from R&P)
Total fees drawn to Supervisor (from R&P)
Nominee fee entitlement under locked model
Supervisor fee entitlement under locked model
Computed cash in hand (contributions - all outflows from R&P)

expected.v15_bug_behaviour: Instructed £696.14 further Nominee draw + £111.77 to creditors (£807.91 total) without verifying cash in hand.
expected.v16_correct_behaviour: Apply waterfall: check fee position, recalculate cash, distribute available cash to creditors. Do not instruct any draw that exceeds available cash.

manual_testing_note: To run this case: supply the full R&P and remaining documents to the /analyze-termination endpoint. Confirm cash_reconciliation.waterfall_triggered = true, instruction_executable = false, ready_to_close = true, and that the final_cashier_instruction does NOT contain a £696.14 Nominee draw. If cash in hand is sufficient to cover the £111.77 shortfall alone, the instruction should be: 'Distribute all remaining funds to creditors' (or waterfall-equivalent wording per the Nominee fee position).
